stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The presence of 'safetensors' indicates modern, secure model handling practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including transformers, llama, and text-generation.
The source, Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicates this is a recognized model within the ML community.

stories.title: Man sues Florida cops over arrest spurred by "93% match" in facial recognition
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: A lawsuit challenges the use of facial recognition technology by Florida police.
The arrest in question was reportedly spurred by a '93% match' from the AI system.
The core legal argument questions the reliance on an 'error-prone AI system' in law enforcement.

stories.title: Which Models Are Our Models Built On? Auditing Invisible Dependencies in Modern LLMs
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: Modern LLM training relies heavily on complex, often undocumented, dependencies on other models for data generation and evaluation.
These dependencies are recursive and fragmented across various public artifacts, making full tracing extremely difficult for humans.
The paper introduces 'ModSleuth,' a tool designed to audit and map these invisible, complex dependency structures within LLM pipelines.

stories.title: German court ruling declares Google's AI Overviews are Google's own words and makes it liable for false answers
stories.author: the-decoder.com via gspr
bullet_points: A German court ruled that Google's AI Overviews are considered Google's own generated content.
The ruling establishes Google's liability for false or inaccurate answers provided by its AI Overviews feature.
This decision signals growing legal scrutiny over the accuracy and responsibility of large language models (LLMs) in search.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization efforts.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, allowing all processing to run on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Amazon’s data centers used 2.5 billion gallons of water last year
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Amazon disclosed its global data center operations consumed 2.5 billion gallons of water last year.
The disclosure comes amid growing public and regulatory scrutiny over water and energy usage in data centers, particularly concerning AI expansion.
The report is relevant to local policy debates, such as the recent one-year data center moratorium enacted in Seattle.

stories.title: FACTR 2: Learning External Force Sensing for Commodity Robot Arms Improves Policy Learning
stories.author: Deepak Pathak
bullet_points: Introduces NEXT (Neural External Torque Estimation), a data-driven method to estimate external joint torques in robot arms.
Crucially, NEXT eliminates the need for expensive, dedicated force sensors, making force-sensitive robotics accessible for low-cost arms.
The method is highly efficient, training quickly (1 minute) using minimal free-motion data (10 minutes) while achieving high accuracy.

stories.title: TAHOE: Text-to-SQL with Automated Hint Optimization from Experience
stories.author: Peng Li
bullet_points: Introduces 'TAHOE,' a system designed to improve Text-to-SQL performance for production environments.
Addresses key challenges in real-world LLM deployment, such as handling strict SQL dialects and massive schemas.
Innovates by treating prompt optimization as a dynamic data management problem using an error-driven hint learning pipeline.

stories.title: FACTR 2: Learning External Force Sensing for Commodity Robot Arms Improves Policy Learning
stories.author: Deepak Pathak
bullet_points: Introduces NEXT, a data-driven method to estimate external joint torques without requiring expensive dedicated force sensors on robot arms.
The method trains rapidly (1 minute) using minimal data (10 minutes of free-motion data) while achieving performance comparable to dedicated sensors.
This innovation enables force-feedback teleoperation and improves policy learning for low-cost robotic manipulators.

stories.title: On Subquadratic Architectures: From Applications to Principles
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: Addresses the computational bottleneck of standard Transformer models due to quadratic attention complexity.
Compares three leading subquadratic architectures (xLSTM, Mamba-2, Gated DeltaNet) as scalable alternatives.
Evaluates these models on complex sequence tasks, including code-model pre-training and distillation.

stories.title: ALIGNBEAM : Inference-Time Alignment Transfer via Cross-Vocabulary Logit Mixing
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Addresses the safety degradation of LLMs when fine-tuned for specific domains, where specialists become vulnerable to harmful prompts.
Introduces ALIGNBEAM, a training-free method to enhance safety by mixing logits from a safe anchor model.
Overcomes a major limitation of existing defenses by allowing logit mixing across models with different vocabularies (cross-family specialists).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.
The tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance within the modern NLP/ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across diverse hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It integrates native support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: The bill that would let Jimmy Kimmel sue Brendan Carr is here
stories.author: Lauren Feiner
bullet_points: A new bipartisan bill, the JAWBONE Act, would allow Americans to sue if a government official illegally coerces a tech company.
The law applies to social media, AI, and broadcasting companies, regardless of whether the content is actually removed.
The bill was introduced by Senators Ted Cruz (R-TX) and Ron Wyden (D-OR) in the Senate Commerce Committee.

stories.title: A warrantless wiretap law is about to expire — but surveillance networks aren’t actually ‘going dark’
stories.author: Gaby Del Valle
bullet_points: Section 702 of FISA, which authorizes warrantless wiretapping, is set to expire due to Congressional inaction.
The House voted against a three-week extension of the controversial surveillance authority.
The article suggests that surveillance networks will not entirely cease operations even if the specific authorization lapses.

stories.title: After nearly breaking, NASA's Deep Space Network "worked well" on Artemis II
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: NASA's Deep Space Network (DSN) successfully functioned during the Artemis II mission despite near-breaking points.
The report suggests that the operational usage of the DSN exceeded documented specifications.
The story highlights the real-world performance and operational stress of critical space infrastructure.

stories.title: "This cannot continue": Xbox leaders lay out "hard truths" behind sagging brand
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Xbox leadership has issued a candid assessment regarding the current state of the gaming division.
The report suggests the Xbox brand is facing significant challenges and a decline in performance.
The article details the 'hard truths' Microsoft is confronting about its gaming strategy.

stories.title: Reroute, Don't Remove: Recoverable Visual Token Routing for Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Yu-Lun Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the computational bottleneck in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) caused by high numbers of visual tokens.
Proposes a novel 'Recoverable Visual Token Routing' method instead of the traditional 'rank-and-remove' paradigm.
Argues that irreversible token removal is fragile because token importance evolves across the decoder's depth.

stories.title: DIRECT: When and Where Should You Allocate Test-Time Compute in Embodied Planners?
stories.author: Marco Pavone
bullet_points: The paper addresses the current trend of scaling test-time compute in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) used for embodied agents.
Over-allocating compute leads to increased latency, token usage, and FLOPs without proportional gains in real-world success.
The core argument is that strategically deciding 'when and where' to allocate compute is crucial for practical deployment of frontier AI.

stories.title: Redesign Mixture-of-Experts Routers with Manifold Power Iteration
stories.author: Yankai Lin
bullet_points: The paper addresses a fundamental design challenge in Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models: effectively designing the router component.
The core problem is the lack of principles to ensure router rows accurately encode expert matrix information for better token-expert affinity.
The proposed solution involves using Manifold Power Iteration to redesign these MoE routers.

stories.title: ATLAS: Active Theory Learning for Automated Science
stories.author: Kevin J. Miller
bullet_points: Introduces ATLAS, an active learning framework designed to automate scientific discovery.
ATLAS focuses on generating interpretable, mechanistic behavioral models from data.
The framework iteratively generates hypotheses using an ensemble of sparse neural networks (Disentangled RNNs).

stories.title: APPO: Agentic Procedural Policy Optimization
stories.author: Xiangxiang Chu
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in current agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods regarding fine-grained credit assignment.
It focuses on two key areas: determining optimal branching points within agent workflows and accurately assigning credit after branching.
The research aims to improve the understanding of how intermediate decisions influence the final outcomes of LLM agents using multi-turn tool-use.

stories.title: Illumination-Robust Camera-Based Heart-Rate Estimation for Physiological Sensing in Robots
stories.author: Torbjörn E. M. Nordling
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of illumination variation in remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) for heart-rate estimation.
It introduces an end-to-end spatial-temporal transformer framework to improve robustness in physiological sensing.
The technology is relevant for enhancing the physiological awareness and interaction capabilities of service and assistive robots.

stories.title: Context-Driven Incremental Compression for Multi-Turn Dialogue Generation
stories.author: Lei Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the computational burden of long dialogue history in conversational AI due to growing attention costs.
Proposes a new context-driven incremental compression method to mitigate information loss during long conversations.
Highlights the limitations of current context compressors, specifically their lack of cross-turn memory sharing and revision capabilities.

stories.title: Redesign Mixture-of-Experts Routers with Manifold Power Iteration
stories.author: Yankai Lin
bullet_points: The paper addresses a fundamental design challenge in Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models: effectively designing the router component.
The proposed method uses 'Manifold Power Iteration' to improve how router rows encode expert information.
The goal is to ensure the router's similarity calculation accurately reflects the affinity between tokens and specific experts.

stories.title: ATLAS: Active Theory Learning for Automated Science
stories.author: Kevin J. Miller
bullet_points: Introduces ATLAS, an active learning framework designed to automate scientific discovery.
ATLAS focuses on generating interpretable, mechanistic behavioral models using data-driven methods.
The framework iterates by creating and testing mechanistic hypotheses instantiated as sparse neural networks (Disentangled RNNs).

stories.title: APPO: Agentic Procedural Policy Optimization
stories.author: Xiangxiang Chu
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods regarding fine-grained credit assignment.
The paper focuses on two key areas: determining optimal branching points within agent workflows and accurately assigning credit post-branching.
It contributes to improving the multi-turn tool-use capabilities of large language model agents.

stories.title: UniIntervene: Agentic Intervention for Efficient Real-World Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Ziwei Wang
bullet_points: Introduces UniIntervene, a new agentic intervention model for Human-in-the-loop Reinforcement Learning (HiL-RL).
Addresses the major limitation of current HiL-RL: high labor costs due to frequent human corrections during policy exploration.
Aims to improve the efficiency and scalability of real-world robotic manipulation by automating intervention.

stories.title: Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
stories.author: Jingren Zhou
bullet_points: The research addresses the bottleneck of slow rollout stages in Reinforcement Learning (RL) training for LLMs.
It focuses on Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) and its performance degradation during RL training.
The proposed solution, 'Bebop,' is a systematic study aimed at improving MTP efficiency in LLM post-training.

stories.title: Latent World Recovery for Multimodal Learning with Missing Modalities
stories.author: Simon McDade
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of multimodal learning when some data modalities are missing.
Proposes Latent World Recovery (LWR), a framework for handling incomplete multimodal data.
Aligns modality-specific embeddings into a shared latent space to construct unified representations.

stories.title: Anatomy of Post-Training: Using Interpretability to Characterize Data and Shape the Learning Signal
stories.author: Ekdeep Singh Lubana
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of visibility into how data shapes language model behavior during post-training.
Current post-training relies heavily on scalar rewards, which abstract away the diverse learning signals present in the data.
The research proposes using interpretability techniques to inspect preference datasets *before* optimization to mitigate issues like spurious correlations and sycophancy.

stories.title: Context-Driven Incremental Compression for Multi-Turn Dialogue Generation
stories.author: Lei Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the computational burden of long dialogue histories in conversational AI due to growing attention costs.
Identifies limitations in current context compression methods, specifically the lack of cross-turn memory sharing and revision.
Proposes a novel 'Context-Driven Incremental Compression' approach to improve efficiency and fidelity in multi-turn dialogue generation.

stories.title: Doc-to-Atom: Learning to Compile and Compose Memory Atoms
stories.author: Srinivas Chappidi
bullet_points: Addresses the quadratic computational cost of attention when processing long input sequences in LLMs.
Proposes 'Doc-to-Atom' to compile and compose 'Memory Atoms' for efficient document understanding.
Builds upon context distillation techniques, aiming to overcome limitations of monolithic adapter approaches.

stories.title: Redesign Mixture-of-Experts Routers with Manifold Power Iteration
stories.author: Yankai Lin
bullet_points: The research addresses a key challenge in Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models: designing effective router components.
The paper proposes using Manifold Power Iteration to redesign MoE routers.
The goal is to improve how router rows encode expert information to better predict token-expert affinity.

stories.title: Verifiable Environments Are LEGO Bricks: Recursive Composition for Reasoning Generalization
stories.author: Dayiheng Liu
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of scaling RL performance in LLMs due to manual construction of verifiable environments.
It introduces RACES (Recursive Automated Composition for Environments) to enable scalable reasoning generalization.
The work focuses on using verifiable environments to enhance the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs) via Reinforcement Learning (RL).

stories.title: Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
stories.author: Jingren Zhou
bullet_points: The research addresses the bottleneck of slow rollout stages in Reinforcement Learning (RL) training for LLMs.
It focuses on Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) and its performance degradation during RL training, which limits speedup.
The proposed solution, 'Bebop,' is a systematic study aimed at improving MTP efficiency in LLM post-training.

stories.title: macOS 27 Beta breaks the ability to boot Asahi Linux
stories.author: josephcsible
bullet_points: A new macOS 27 Beta version has introduced a critical issue.
The update reportedly breaks the functionality required to boot Asahi Linux.
This highlights ongoing compatibility challenges between Apple's ecosystem and alternative OS implementations.

stories.title: Why New Language Features Need to Target AI Agents, Not Developers
stories.author: moderncpp
bullet_points: The article argues that language design should shift focus from human developers to AI agents.
The premise is that AI agents are increasingly capable of writing production code.
It calls for optimizing programming language syntax specifically for bot consumption rather than human convenience.

stories.title: You Fixed the Rate Limits. Now Your Agent Fails Quietly.
stories.author: p0rt
bullet_points: The article discusses the difference between basic uptime and 'correct uptime' in system design.
It critiques common capacity fixes (retries, caching) for potentially masking underlying issues rather than solving them.
The core theme is engineering for correctness beyond mere availability, especially in AI/agent systems.
